question: The water quality of a pond was analysed and its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> was found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math>. The pond has
options: Water has high amount of fluoride compounds
solution:
The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water has BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas polluted water has a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

So, the pond has very clean water.

question:
Which of the following statement(s) is (are)
incorrect reason for eutrophication?
(A) excess usage of fertilisers
(B) excess usage of detergents
(C) dense plant population in water bodies
(D) lack of nutrients in water bodies that prevent plant growth

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution: The process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as eutrophication.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Some reactions of NO2 relevant to photochemical smog formation are

Identify A,B,X and Y
question:
Some reactions of NO2 relevant to photochemical smog formation are

Identify A,B,X and Y
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. Two of the pollutants that are emitted are hydrocarbons (unburnt fuels) and nitric oxide (NO). When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide NO2. This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and a free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g) →hν NO (g) + O(g)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ O3 (g)

Hence, X = O, Y = NO, A = O2, B = O3

solution: Photochemical smog consists of nitrogen oxides, volatile organic compounds, tropospheric ozone, etc., <math><mtext>C</mtext><msub><mrow><mtext>F</mtext></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mtext>C</mtext><msub><mrow><mtext>l</mtext></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> , a chlorofluorocarbon, is not present in photochemical smog, but is a major cause of ozone-layer depletion.

subject: Chemistry
q: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
question: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
solution: Wet deposition is the acidic water received through rain, fog, snow, dew, mist, etc. Dry deposition is deposition of wind blown acidic gases and particles on the ground. Ammonium salt in rain drop result in wet deposition. Oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur settle down on ground as dry deposition.

subject: Chemistry
q: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
question: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
solution: The concentration of dissolved oxygen in water is very important for aquatic life. If the concentration of dissolved oxygen of water is below 6 ppm, the growth of fish gets inhibited. The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

subject: Chemistry
q: The eutrophication of water body results in
question: The eutrophication of water body results in
solution: The addition of phosphates in water enhances algae growth. Such profuse growth of algae, covers the water surface and reduces the oxygen concentration in water. This leads to anaerobic conditions, commonly with accumulation of abnoxious decay and animal death. Thus, bloom-infested water inhibits the growth of other living organisms in the water body. This process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population, which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as Eutrophication.

q:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide (NO2). This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g)→hvNO (g)+O (g)  A B-----(i)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g)+O2 (g)⇌O3 (g)B   C-----(ii)

The ozone formed in the above reaction (ii) reacts rapidly with the NO (g) formed in reaction (i) to regenerate NO2 . NO2 is a brown gas and at sufficiently high levels can contribute to haze.

NO (g)+O3 (g)→NO2 (g)+A C   O2 (g) ----(iii)

So, the A, B and C are NO, O and O3 respectively.

subject: Chemistry
q: BOD stands for:
question: BOD stands for:
solution:
Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) is also called Biological Oxygen Demand.

It is the amount of dissolved oxygen needed (i.e. demanded) by aerobic biological organisms to break down organic material present in a given water sample at certain temperature over a specific time period

The problem of ozone depletion is more serious at poles because ice crystals in the clouds over poles act as catalyst for photochemical reactions involving the decomposition of ozone of $\mathrm{Cl}^{\bullet}$ and $\mathrm{CIO}^{\bullet}$ radicals.
Freons, chlorofluorocarbons, are inert. Chemically, they do not react with ozone in stratosphere.
Oxides of nitrogen also do not react with ozone in stratosphere.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
question: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
solution:
Chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) are non-reactive, non-flammable molecules that are used in refrigerators, air conditioners, plastics, and electronic industries. Once released CFCs mix with atmospheric gases and reach the stratosphere, where they are decomposed by UV radiations.

CF2Cl2g→uvCl⊙g+C⊙F2Clg

The chlorine radical released will react with ozone.

Cl⊙g+O3g→O2g+ClOg⊙

The Cl⊙O will generate chlorine radicals again

ClOg⊙+Og→O2g+Cl⊙

q: <math><mn>10.30</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is dissolved into a liter of sea water of density <math><mn>1.03</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> . The concentration of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> in ppm is _____________.
question: <math><mn>10.30</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is dissolved into a liter of sea water of density <math><mn>1.03</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> . The concentration of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> in ppm is _____________.
solution:
Formula of ppm is-

ppm=weight of soluteweight of solution×106

ppm=10.3×10-31030×106 =10

PPM is a concentration term and its full form is "parts per million" and it also can be expressed as milligrams per liter (mg/L). This measurement is the mass of a chemical or contaminate per unit volume of water.

subject: Chemistry
q: The photochemical smog does not generally contain
question: The photochemical smog does not generally contain
solution:
The major components of photochemical smog are nitrogen oxides, ozone O3, PAN (peroxyacetylnitrate), and chemical compounds that contain the-CHO group (aldehydes). PAN and aldehydes can cause eye irritation and plant damage if their concentrations are sufficiently high.

Classical smog occurs in a cool, humid climate. Its components include smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide. It is reducing in nature.

subject: Chemistry
q: In stratosphere most of the ozone formation is assisted by :
question: In stratosphere most of the ozone formation is assisted by :
solution:
Ozone in the stratosphere is a product of UV radiations acting on dioxygen O2 molecules.

O2( g)⟶UVO(g)+O(g)

O(g)+O2( g)⇌UVO3( g)

(C) Methane is a stronger global warming gas than CO2
(D) Methane is a part of reducing smog

solution: Methane leads to both global warming & photochemical smog. Methane is generated in large amounts from paddy fields. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> can be absorbed by photosynthesis, or by formation of acid rain etc., while no such activities are there for methane. Hence methane is stronger global warming gas than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, Methane is not a component of reducing smog.

subject: Chemistry
q: Water filled in two glasses A and B gave <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> values of <math><mn>10</mn></math> and <math><mn>20</mn><mo>,</mo></math> respectively. The correct statement regarding them is
question: Water filled in two glasses A and B gave <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> values of <math><mn>10</mn></math> and <math><mn>20</mn><mo>,</mo></math> respectively. The correct statement regarding them is
options: A is suitable for drinking, whereas B is not
B is more polluted than A
Both A and B are suitable for drinking
A is more polluted than B
solution: A water supply with a BOD level of 3-5 ppm is considered moderately clean. In water with a BOD level of 6-9 ppm, the water is considered somewhat polluted because there is usually organic matter present and bacteria are decomposing this waste. Hence, Both A and B are not suitable for drinking. B with a higher value of BOD level is more polluted.

q: The higher concentration of which gas in air can cause stiffnes of flower buds?
question: The higher concentration of which gas in air can cause stiffnes of flower buds?
solution: High concentration of $\mathrm{SO}_{2}$ leads to stiffness of flower buds.
